mechanism_narrative: When the effective federal funds rate exceeds a high threshold, restrictive monetary policy compresses equity valuations by increasing the discount rate and slowing economic activity, leading to negative forward returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Current FEDFUNDS is 5.33, the highest since 2007.
failure_condition: S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when FEDFUNDS is above 5.0% over a 5-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained positive momentum in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a robust and expanding economy, which translates to higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Recent payroll reports have shown continued, albeit slowing, job growth.
failure_condition: The 3-month rolling correlation between the 6-month change in PAYEMS and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is consistently negative or statistically insignificant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When strong retail sales growth occurs simultaneously with high inflation, it signals an overheating economy. Markets anticipate a hawkish monetary policy response, leading to a contraction in multiples and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: Year-over-year change in RSAFS > 3% AND Year-over-year change in CPIAUCSL > 3%
evidence_refs: fact:Inflation remains above the Fed's target while consumer spending has been resilient.
failure_condition: In periods where both trigger conditions are met, the average 60-day forward S&P 500 return is non-negative over a 10-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: A negative rate of change in industrial production signals a contraction in the manufacturing and supply side of the economy, which is a leading indicator of a broader economic slowdown and subsequent decline in corporate profits, causing negative equity returns.
evidence_refs: fact:Recent industrial production data has shown signs of flattening or slight decline.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between the 3-month change in INDPRO and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is greater than zero over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate from its 12-month low is a reliable real-time indicator of a recession ('Sahm Rule' intuition). This structural shift in the labor market leads to sharply lower earnings expectations and negative equity returns.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE is > 0.5 percentage points above its 12-month minimum
evidence_refs: fact:Unemployment has ticked up to 3.9% from a low of 3.4% in early 2023.
failure_condition: In the 60 trading days following a trigger event, S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 40% of historical instances.

mechanism_narrative: An acceleration in the month-over-month change of the Consumer Price Index (CPI) signals persistent inflationary pressures, increasing the likelihood of further monetary tightening by the central bank and negatively impacting equity valuations in the short term.
evidence_refs: fact:Recent CPI prints have been closely watched for signs of re-acceleration.
failure_condition: The correlation between the 1-month change in CPIAUCSL and 5-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive or statistically indistinguishable from zero over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in housing starts (HOUST) is a leading indicator of weakening consumer demand and tighter financial conditions, which precedes broader economic weakness and negatively affects forward equity returns.
candidate_trigger: 3-month moving average of HOUST is below the 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:Housing activity has slowed significantly due to higher mortgage rates.
failure_condition: When the trigger condition is met, the average 20-day forward S&P 500 return is positive over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: In a high interest rate environment (FEDFUNDS > 4%), continued strength in the labor market (low UNRATE) is interpreted negatively by markets, as it implies the economy can withstand further tightening, increasing the probability of a 'higher for longer' policy stance.
evidence_refs: fact:The Fed has explicitly linked its policy path to labor market conditions.
failure_condition: When FEDFUNDS > 4%, the correlation between UNRATE levels and 20-day forward S&P 500 returns is positive and statistically significant (p < 0.05).
